stories.title: Project Setup Command (/setup)
stories.description: As a developer I want to initialize all project starting files with the complete documentation structure So that I can start a new project with a standardized, consistent foundation.

stories.title: Review and Setup Command for Legacy Projects (/review-and-setup)
stories.description: As a developer I want to reverse-engineer documentation from existing codebases So that I can bring legacy projects into the standardized documentation workflow.

stories.title: Define Command for Document Elaboration (/define)
stories.description: As a developer I want to define or refine a specific documentation file with AI assistance So that I can produce well-structured, context-aware documentation quickly.

stories.title: Progress Tracking System (/update-progress)
stories.description: As a developer I want to automatically track feature completion aligned with atomic stories So that I have real-time visibility into project health and velocity.

stories.title: ADR Management (/create-adr)
stories.description: As a developer I want to create Architectural Decision Records with automatic indexing So that I can document and track significant architectural decisions over time.

stories.title: Build Orchestrator Command (/build)
stories.description: As a developer I want an intelligent orchestrator that detects pending UACs and executes build commands in parallel or sequentially So that I can scaffold implementations efficiently without manual coordination.

stories.title: Build Skill Meta-Command (/build-skill)
stories.description: As a developer I want to create new Claude Code Skills with proper structure and formatting So that I can extend the system with reusable domain-specific capabilities.

stories.title: Build Frontend Command (/build-fe)
stories.description: As a developer I want to scaffold frontend implementation with unit and E2E tests based on specifications So that I can rapidly build production-ready React/Next.js frontends.

stories.title: Build Backend Command (/build-be)
stories.description: As a developer I want to scaffold backend implementation with unit and integration tests based on specifications So that I can rapidly build production-ready NestJS/Node.js backends.

stories.title: Build DevOps Command (/build-devops)
stories.description: As a developer I want to scaffold DevOps infrastructure and CI/CD pipelines with comprehensive testing So that I can deploy reliably with automated quality gates.

stories.title: Convert to Antigravity Command (/convert-to-antigravity)
stories.description: As a developer I want to convert Claude Skills to Google Antigravity Workflows So that I can achieve cross-IDE compatibility for all skills.

stories.title: Release Management Command (/release)
stories.description: As a developer I want to create and manage project releases with automatic version tagging and release notes So that I can deliver consistent, well-documented releases.

stories.title: New Feature Command (/new-feature)
stories.description: As a developer I want to add new feature requirements to the latest PRD and Atomic Stories So that I can extend the product roadmap with proper documentation.

stories.title: Define Draft Mode (/define --draft)
stories.description: As a developer I want to create draft versions of documents for isolated elaboration So that I can work on document revisions without affecting production documentation.

stories.title: Define Finalize (/define --finalize)
stories.description: As a developer I want to finalize draft documents by removing the draft suffix and updating cross-references So that draft work can be promoted to production cleanly.

stories.title: Design Research Command (/design --research)
stories.description: As a developer I want to research UI designs with AI assistance, generating design options with mockups So that I can make informed design decisions quickly.

stories.title: Design Planning Command (/design --plan)
stories.description: As a developer I want to plan component and feature integration to avoid breaking changes So that I can safely evolve the frontend architecture.

stories.title: Design Storybook Command (/design --storybook)
stories.description: As a developer I want to create versioned Storybook examples with component status tracking So that I can document and showcase UI components systematically.

stories.title: Design Update Frontend Command (/design --update-fe)
stories.description: As a developer I want to update frontend components with PR creation and validation So that I can maintain component quality and traceability.

stories.title: Google Analytics Planning Command (/google-analytics --plan)
stories.description: As a developer I want to create a Google Analytics implementation plan with event tracking strategy and GA4 configuration So that I can measure user behavior with a structured analytics approach.

stories.title: Google Analytics Implementation Command (/google-analytics --implement)
stories.description: As a developer I want to generate analytics tracking code with tests based on an analytics plan So that I can implement GA4 tracking with confidence and test coverage.

stories.title: Learning Documentation Command
stories.description: As a developer or team lead I want to capture and document learnings from development, debugging, and implementations So that I can prevent repeating mistakes and preserve organizational knowledge for future reference.
stories.category: Knowledge Management, Quality Assurance & Business Intelligence

uacs.description: Command accepts learning description
Prompts user to select domain tag (FE, BE, DevOps, DB, Testing, Design, Architecture)
Creates learning document in /learnings folder
Filename format: {YYYYMMDD}-{domain}-{short-desc}.md
Structured template with sections: Context, Problem, Solution, Lessons Learned, Related Documents
Supports multiple domain tags for cross-cutting learnings
Creates /learnings/000-README.md index if it doesn't exist
Updates index with new learning entry (auto-numbering like ADRs)
Searchable by domain tag, date, or keywords
Cross-references to related ADRs and documentation
Can attach code snippets or error logs
Integrates with progress tracking (optional milestone: Knowledge Base Milestone)
Provides summary of learning and suggestions for related readings

stories.notes: New document category: /learnings folder. Similar structure to ADRs but focused on lessons learned. Auto-indexing like ADR system. Cross-domain tagging support.

stories.title: Code Review Command
stories.description: As a developer or tech lead I want to perform systematic code reviews for branches or PRs with quality standards validation So that I can maintain consistent code quality and catch issues before they reach production.
stories.category: Knowledge Management, Quality Assurance & Business Intelligence

uacs.description: Uses git to analyze branch diffs
Command accepts branch name and optional base branch (defaults to main)
Analyzes git diff between branches
Validates test coverage (checks tests exist, runs tests, calculates coverage, identifies files without tests)
Checks SOLID principles (SRP, OCP, LSP, ISP, DIP)
Detects code smells (long methods, large classes, duplication, cyclomatic complexity, magic numbers, commented-out code, TODOs)
Validates best practices (error handling, security, performance, naming, code style)
Checks documentation (README, API docs, component docs, comments for complex logic)
Interactive review with AskUserQuestion showing findings by severity (Critical, High, Medium, Low)
For each finding: Fix now? Create issue? Document as exception? Ignore?
Generates comprehensive review report (executive summary, findings by severity, test coverage, action items, recommendations)
Creates GitHub issue for each Create issue decision
Documents exceptions in code review log
Integrates with progress tracking

stories.notes: Most complex story in v1.4.0 (21 points). Requires git diff analysis. Interactive workflow with AskUserQuestion. Multiple quality checks (tests, SOLID, code smells, best practices). GitHub integration for issue creation.

stories.title: Monetization Planning Command
stories.description: As a product owner or business stakeholder I want to analyze costs and create pricing strategies for my SaaS product So that I can make informed business decisions and ensure profitability.
stories.category: Knowledge Management, Quality Assurance & Business Intelligence

uacs.description: Analyzes infrastructure requirements for cost estimation
Command supports two modes: --analyze (cost analysis) and --strategy (pricing strategy)
Creates document docs/500-monetization-v*.md
Cost Analysis Mode: reads infrastructure requirements, estimates cloud costs, calculates development/operational/third-party costs, provides per-user cost
Pricing Strategy Mode: analyzes competitor pricing, suggests pricing models (cost-plus, value-based, competitive, freemium), recommends tiered pricing, calculates break-even
Supports --update flag to refresh cost/pricing analysis
Integrates with progress tracking
Provides comprehensive monetization document

stories.notes: New document category: 500-series (Business Intelligence). Dual-mode operation (--analyze and --strategy). Reads infrastructure requirements from docs/425-devops. Provides multiple pricing model recommendations.

stories.title: POC Building Command
stories.description: As a developer or architect I want to build proof-of-concept implementations to validate architectural decisions So that I can validate assumptions before committing to full implementation.
stories.category: Knowledge Management, Quality Assurance & Business Intelligence

uacs.description: Generates frontend POC code if format is vanilla or node
Generates backend POC code if format is node or python
Sets up database if needed for POC
Creates isolated POC branch with structure
Command accepts POC description and format flag (--format notebook|vanilla|node|python)
Creates POC branch: pocs/{short-description}
Creates POC documentation: /pocs/{NNN}-poc-{short-desc}.md with Objectives, Success Criteria, Implementation Notes, Results, Recommendations, Related Documents
Generates minimal POC code structure for each format
Prompts user for success criteria during POC creation
Tracks POC status (In Progress, Completed, Abandoned)
After POC completion, prompts to create ADR, update architecture docs, or convert to /new-feature
Creates /pocs/000-README.md index if doesn't exist and updates with POC entry
Provides POC workflow guidance

stories.notes: Complex workflow with multiple POC formats. Integrated with ADR system for decision documentation. Can trigger architecture document updates. Branch-based isolation for POCs. New folder: /pocs with index system.

stories.title: Landing Page Generation Command
stories.description: As a product owner or marketer I want to generate conversion-optimized landing pages aligned with product strategy So that I can funnel users to my application with effective marketing materials.
stories.category: Knowledge Management, Quality Assurance & Business Intelligence

uacs.description: Generates responsive landing page component (React/Next.js)
Includes mobile-responsive CSS (Tailwind)
Generates unit tests for landing page
Generates E2E tests for form submissions and CTAs
Command accepts optional persona and framework flags
Reads context from PRD, user flows, atomic stories, design system
Supports multiple landing page frameworks (Hormozi, AIDA, PAS, BAB, FAB)
Persona-specific content generation tailored to pain points
Generates landing page sections (Hero, Value Prop, Features, Social Proof, Pricing, FAQ, Final CTA)
Generates A/B testing variants (2-3 versions with different headlines/CTAs)
Integrates GA4 tracking (CTA clicks, form submissions, scroll depth)
Creates design documentation in /design/web/pages/ and HTML mockup
Provides conversion optimization checklist

stories.notes: Multiple landing page frameworks (Hormozi, AIDA, PAS, etc.). Persona-specific content generation. A/B testing variant generation. Integrated with design system and GA4 analytics. Conversion optimization focus.

stories.title: Strategy Building Command
stories.description: As a product owner or developer I want to create, store, and track versioned strategy documents with AI assistance So that I can make informed decisions grounded in project context and answer open strategic questions.
stories.category: Knowledge Management, Quality Assurance & Business Intelligence

uacs.description: Command is /strategy [description]
Uses AskUserQuestion tool to propose 3 file name options
Creates /strategy/ folder if it doesn't exist
File naming format: {NNN}-{kebab-case-name}-v{version}.md
Auto-increments the NNN prefix based on existing files in /strategy/
Each strategy document includes title, version, date, change log, open questions, context, analysis, recommendations, decision log
Reads context from latest PRD, user flows, design system, and C4 architecture diagram docs
Auto-creates and switches to strategy/{name} git branch before writing files
Never commits to main/default branch
Keeps /strategy/README.md up to date as TOC with abstracts
Paired with /create-pr for submitting the strategy branch as a PR

stories.notes: All original UACs implemented; skill significantly enhanced beyond original spec. Porter's Five Forces analysis serves as first real-world test. PR merged to main February 18, 2026.

stories.title: Create Pull Request Command
stories.description: As a developer or strategist I want to create a GitHub pull request for my current branch with a well-structured description So that I can submit my changes for review without leaving the Claude Code environment.
stories.category: Knowledge Management, Quality Assurance & Business Intelligence

uacs.description: Command is /create-pr [--base branch]
Defaults --base to the repository's default branch (main or master) if not provided
Reads the current branch name via git branch --show-current
Uses gh pr create via the Bash tool to create the PR
Auto-generates a PR title from the branch name
Auto-generates a PR body with Summary, Files Changed, Test Plan, link to strategy/docs file if present
Reads recent git commits on the branch for context
Outputs the PR URL after creation
Errors gracefully if not authenticated, no commits ahead, or PR already exists
Can be used standalone or paired with /strategy

stories.notes: Skill file: .claude/skills/create-pr/skill.md (449 lines, 5 phases). First manual test: created PR #2 for the skill itself (feat/create-pr-skill → main).

stories.title: Enhanced Strategy Command (Framework Analysis + Realign)
stories.description: As a product owner, developer, or strategist I want to create framework-specific strategy documents (5 Whys bilateral analysis, Porter's 5 Forces) and realign all existing strategy documents when the project's strategic direction evolves So that I can conduct structured analytical work using proven frameworks and keep all strategy documents consistent over time.
stories.category: Knowledge Management, Quality Assurance & Business Intelligence

uacs.description: No-args invocation uses two-question menu (General vs. Framework-based vs. Other)
--5-whys flag with bilateral Identity/Beliefs/Systems/Patterns/Events template
--5-whys prompts for problem statement and anchor side (both/product/user/not sure)
--5-whys auto-generates five-whys-{topic} name candidates (topic max 30 chars)
5 Whys template: 5 levels × two-column table, Root Cause Synthesis table, Root Cause Category checkboxes, Corrective Actions table
--porters-5-forces flag with Porter's template (5 forces × Score 1-5, Overall Position, Strategic Implications)
Porter's auto-generates porters-five-forces-{topic} name candidates (topic max 20 chars)
Both framework flags run Phase 2-7 (context reading, goals/metrics/experiments, branch creation, validation, summary)
Phase 6 validation uses template-mode-aware section checklists
Phase 7 summary displays Framework: line
--realign triggers separate workflow (Phases R0-R8): reads all strategy docs, synthesizes identity, finds contradictions, creates new minor-version files, optionally updates PRD

stories.notes: Enhancement to Story 315 (/strategy). All three modes validated: --5-whys, --porters-5-forces, and --realign. Manual tests run in external repository February 18, 2026. PR #3 merged to main.

stories.title: Project Dashboard with /sync-board
stories.description: As a developer or product owner I want a visual project dashboard that shows all atomic stories, their statuses, UACs, metrics, and decision-making suggestions generated from project documentation So that I can track project health, identify bottlenecks, and make informed prioritization decisions without leaving my local environment.
stories.category: Knowledge Management, Quality Assurance & Business Intelligence

uacs.description: /sync-board reads latest docs/200-atomic-stories-v*.md (highest version, excluding drafts)
/sync-board reads latest docs/progress/000-progress-v*.md (highest version)
Cross-references story IDs between atomic stories and progress doc to build unified story objects
Generates backend/src/data/board.json with schema: { metadata, stories, releases, metrics }
stories[] items include: id, title, description, status, effort, priority, version, category, dependencies, uacs[], notes
uacs[] items include: tag, description, status (completed/pending/blocked)
releases[] groups completed stories by version with version, name, released_at, stories[], key_achievements[]
metrics includes: total_stories, completed, in_progress, pending, blocked, completion_pct, points_completed, points_remaining, points_by_version, velocity_per_sprint, estimated_remaining_sprints
metrics.suggestions[] — decision-making recommendations (available/bottleneck/high-value/stale types)
metadata includes: generated_at, atomic_stories_version, progress_version, total_stories, overall_completion_pct
Creates backend/src/data/ directory if it does not exist
Supports --dry-run flag: prints JSON to stdout without writing to disk
Prints success summary on completion
React application in /frontend folder (Vite + React 18 + TypeScript + Tailwind CSS)
Main board view: kanban-style columns (Pending / In Progress / Completed / Blocked)
Story card displays: ID badge, title, priority badge, effort badge, version tag
Story detail panel with full UAC list grouped by tag with status icons
Metrics panel: overall completion %, story point progress bar, per-version progress bars
Suggestions section from metrics.suggestions[] with type badges
Released Features section grouped by version with key achievements
Filter bar (by version, by status) and search input (client-side)
Manual refresh button; loading skeleton; 503 error state with /sync-board instructions
Responsive layout: desktop primary (>=1024px), tablet supported (>=768px)
NestJS application in /backend folder (NestJS 10+, TypeScript)
GET /api/board returns full board.json content
GET /api/stories with query params ?version= and ?status=
GET /api/releases returns releases array
GET /api/metrics returns metrics with suggestions
GET /health health check endpoint
CORS enabled for all origins (local development)
HTTP 503 with helpful message when board.json not found
Swagger/OpenAPI at /api/docs
No authentication required (local-only design)

stories.notes: New /sync-board Claude Code skill required. New /frontend React app (Vite + Tailwind CSS). New /backend NestJS server. Architecture docs created: 300-frontend-v1.1.0.md, 325-backend-v1.1.0.md, 350-api-contract-v1.1.0.md. ADR created: docs/adr/20260218-project-dashboard-react-nestjs.md.

releases.name: MVP
key_achievements: Complete documentation system with 17+ file templates
Reverse-engineering documentation from existing codebases
AI-assisted document elaboration with context aggregation
Automatic progress tracking with velocity calculations
ADR creation with auto-indexing
Intelligent build orchestration with parallel execution

releases.name: Code Generation Toolkit
key_achievements: Meta-skill for creating new Claude Skills (522 lines)
Frontend code generation with tests (1,508 lines)
Backend code generation with tests (1,928 lines)
DevOps infrastructure generation (1,491 lines)
80% test coverage enforcement across all layers
6,126+ lines of code generation tooling

releases.name: Cross-IDE Compatibility & QA
key_achievements: Cross-IDE compatibility achieved (14 workflows)
Automated release management with documentation tracking
100% character limit compliance for Antigravity workflows

releases.name: Draft Workflow & Design System
key_achievements: Complete draft workflow system
Full design system tooling from research to production
Analytics integration with privacy compliance (GDPR/CCPA)
Versioned component management with Storybook
GA4 tracking with 27+ comprehensive tests

suggestions.title: Story 310 is ready to start
suggestions.detail: No unmet dependencies (001, 003 completed). High priority knowledge management story in v1.4.0. 13 story points.

suggestions.title: Story 311 is ready to start
suggestions.detail: No unmet dependencies (001, 003, 004 completed). Highest-effort story in v1.4.0 at 21 points. Start early to avoid schedule risk.

suggestions.title: Story 313 is ready to start
suggestions.detail: No unmet dependencies (001, 003, 005 completed). High priority POC building command. 21 story points.

suggestions.title: Story 311 (Code Review) is high-value and unblocked
suggestions.detail: 21 story points, high priority, no unmet dependencies. Most complex story in v1.4.0 — strong ROI for next sprint. Delivers systematic code review with SOLID principles validation.

suggestions.title: Story 318 (Project Dashboard) is high-value and unblocked
suggestions.detail: 21 story points, high priority, no unmet dependencies (001, 003, 004 completed). Delivers full React + NestJS dashboard with real-time project metrics and decision-making suggestions.

suggestions.title: Story 312 is ready to start
suggestions.detail: No unmet dependencies (001, 003 completed). Medium priority monetization planning command. 13 story points.

suggestions.title: Story 314 is ready to start
suggestions.detail: No unmet dependencies (001, 003, 304 all completed). Medium priority landing page generation command. 13 story points.
